response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong full-stack and DLang depth, plus some Java exposure (LabWindows/CVI era C/C++ and robotic arms with Java at Altom). However, the role demands deep JVM, Kotlin, and Spring/Hibernate/Quarkus expertise with a genuine interest in IDE internals — none of which appear meaningfully in the resume. Recent work is TypeScript/Node and AI tooling, not enterprise JVM. Misalignment outweighs the transferable systems experience.
strengths: Long track record on complex, large-scale systems (Sociomantic micro-services, GISCollective platform)
Prior IDE-adjacent work on LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments shows exposure to developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), workshops
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java history; recent stack is TypeScript/Node
No Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or application-server experience listed
No evidence of deep JVM internals or modern IDE/language-tooling work
